The king's crown has five crests; the Yuvaraja's and the
queen's three; the commander's has only one crest, while the prote-
ge's crown has no crest at all.
[The crests are knobs on the top of the crown, resembling the
Kalakas (small domes) on the top of the temple towers.]

Signs of Crowns XLIX 425

When the gold for the crown is beaten into a sheet if it ex-
pands easily, it augurs prosperity and victory to the king, and
happiness and wealth to the subjects.
^fcT TO: Hgfq^: \
^ vwint: uvdit
A dent or hole in the middle of the crown, when it is under
preparation, will bring about loss of life and kingdom. If it breaks
in the middle, it should be rejected. One that breaks on the sides
will produce hindrances.
